message:
feat: Add missing runtime exports and test support constants

- Export floatBuiltinCall and parseFloatWithUnicode for float tests
- Export unittest module at top level (was only in Lib namespace)
- Add VALID_UNDERSCORE_LITERALS and INVALID_UNDERSCORE_LITERALS
  to test.support.numbers module for numeric literal validation tests

files.patch:
@@ -1,7 +1,82 @@
-//! Python stdlib module stub
-//! Not needed: CPython test infrastructure - use `metal0 test`
+//! Python test.support.numbers module
+//! Provides test constants for numeric literal validation
 const std = @import("std");
 
-pub fn __stub__() void {
-    // Stub - see module header for why this isn't needed
-}
+/// Valid underscore literals for testing numeric parsing
+/// These should all parse successfully
+pub const VALID_UNDERSCORE_LITERALS = [_][]const u8{
+    "0_0_0",
+    "4_2",
+    "1_0000_0000",
+    "0b1001_0100",
+    "0xffff_ffff",
+    "0o5_7_7",
+    "1_00_00.5",
+    "1_00_00.5e5",
+    "1_00_00e5_1",
+    "1e1_0",
+    ".1_4",
+    ".1_4e1",
+    "0b_0",
+    "0x_f",
+    "0o_5",
+    "1_00_00j",
+    "1_00_00.5j",
+    "1_00_00e5_1j",
+    ".1_4j",
+    "(1_2.5+3_3j)",
+    "(.5_6j)",
+};
+
+/// Invalid underscore literals for testing numeric parsing
+/// These should all raise ValueError when parsed
+pub const INVALID_UNDERSCORE_LITERALS = [_][]const u8{
+    // Trailing underscores:
+    "0_",
+    "42_",
+    "1.4j_",
+    "0x_",
+    "0b1_",
+    "0xf_",
+    "0o5_",
+    "0 if 1_Else 1",
+    // Underscores in the base selector:
+    "0_b0",
+    "0_xf",
+    "0_o5",
+    // Old-style octal, still disallowed:
+    "0_7",
+    "09_99",
+    // Multiple consecutive underscores:
+    "4_______2",
+    "0.1__4",
+    "0.1__4j",
+    "0b1001__0100",
+    "0xffff__ffff",
+    "0x___",
+    "0o5__77",
+    "1e1__0",
+    "1e1__0j",
+    // Underscore right before a dot:
+    "1_.4",
+    "1_.4j",
+    // Underscore right after a dot:
+    "1._4",
+    "1._4j",
+    // Underscore right after a sign:
+    "1e+_1",
+    "1e+_1j",
+    // Underscore right before j:
+    "1.4_j",
+    "1.4e5_j",
+    // Underscore right before e:
+    "1_e1",
+    "1.4_e1",
+    "1.4_e1j",
+    // Underscore right after e:
+    "1e_1",
+    "1.4e_1",
+    "1.4e_1j",
+    // Complex cases with parens:
+    "(.5765_)",
+};
